Five-minute completion-gated profile

GLM-5.2 Prefill Kernel Timeline

A PP=8, one-token standalone prefill stress test on gpu-worker-204. Requests and Nsight collection overlap for five minutes; profiler stop occurs only after every stream reaches EOF, result rows are durable, and all scheduler ranks are idle.

4,282 / 4,282 completed0 failures0 decode batchescomplete scheduler logsgenerated 2026-07-10T15:00:44.326085+08:00
301,155prompt tokens/s with repeated 14-record corpus
90.41Mprompt tokens across 4,282 streams
300.22smeasured client wall time
11.33MGPU kernel events across eight devices
249.68 GPU-snon-NCCL aggregate residency
20.05 GPU-sNCCL SendRecv aggregate residency
0.508smean TTFT; p99 1.756s
363.20sfull capture: pre-roll + 300.19s measured + gate
Result. The five-minute run sustained 301,155.34 prompt tok/s with 14.26 completed requests/s. This includes radix-cache reuse from the deliberately repeated corpus; it is not cold unique-prompt throughput.

Exact Run Contract

Worker side

FieldValue
Node / GPUsgpu-worker-204 / 8 x NVIDIA B200
Imageimage.hpc-ai.com/platform/dynamo-sglang-runtime:b68202c4-dev
ParallelismTP=1, DP=1, PP=8
Token pool / context1,048,576 / 1,048,576
Chunk / max prefill16,384 / 16,384
Max running48
GEMM / MoEcutlass / cutlass
KV cachefp8_e4m3, page size 64
Request logging--log-requests-level 1; metadata retained without full prompt bodies
CUDA graphsDecode disabled; no graph IDs captured

Request side

FieldValue
Modeprefill-standalone, native /generate
Corpusdata/cc, 14 files / 14 records
Users16 closed-loop sessions, spawned at 2/s
Issue window300s; active request drained before client exit
Between requestsIndependent uniform(0.2, 1.0)s wait per session
Prompt choiceIndependent uniform choice among all 14 records for every request
Outputmax_new_tokens=1, ignore_eos=true, temperature 0
Result20260710_142956_local_zai-org-glm-5.2_local_16u

Only gpu-worker-204 was used. The standard keeps the 1,048,576-token pool/context, 16,384-token chunk, and 48-running-request settings.

Request Shape And Session Timeline

This workload is closed loop, not a fixed global RPS stream. Each of 16 simulated users owns one sequential session. A session never has two requests in flight: its next send is causally gated by completion of its current stream plus a random think-time wait.

1 · SpawnTwo sessions per second start until all 16 exist. The first pair sends at t=0.028s; the final pair appears near t=7s.
2 · SelectThe session independently chooses one of 14 prompt records with uniform probability. Choice is repeated afresh for every request.
3 · SendThe chosen prompt is posted to native /generate with one requested output token. The colored lane interval begins here.
4 · CompleteFirst-token time is effectively request completion because there is exactly one generated token. Stream EOF follows by only transport overhead.
5 · WaitAfter EOF, that session waits a fresh uniform 0.2-1.0s, then returns to Select. At 300s no new request is issued; in-flight work drains.
Prompt reuse semantics. A user is not pinned to one prompt and does not walk the corpus in order. Every selection is independent, so immediate repeats are possible. Across this long run, every session selected all 14 records and reused 245-261 previously seen records. Reuse is both within a session and across sessions through the server's shared radix cache.
P0 · 61,102 tokensP1 · 6,239 tokensP2 · 41,878 tokensP3 · 41,764 tokensP4 · 2,429 tokensP5 · 67,065 tokensP6 · 28,521 tokensP7 · 14,193 tokensP8 · 14,224 tokensP9 · 5,608 tokensP10 · 2,433 tokensP11 · 2,502 tokensP12 · 2,708 tokensP13 · 2,533 tokens

The run contains 4,282 colored intervals. The last request begins at client t=299.787890s and the last stream reaches EOF at t=300.219226s. Peak in-flight work is 16 requests at t=10.285s. Hovering any interval shows its user, sequence number, prompt identity, token count, send, first-token, and EOF timestamps.

Kernel Timeline

The green band is the exact measured window, from first request send at profiler t=11.379s through final EOF at t=311.570s. The short pre-roll establishes collection before issuance; the post-run region preserves the completion and idle-server gate.

What residency means

Kernel residency is the sum of Nsight GPU event-interval overlap. Every kernel interval is clipped at one-second bin boundaries, and its overlapping duration is added to that bin. This is not CUDA occupancy, useful FLOPs, request latency, or wall-clock duration. NCCL kernels may remain resident while communicating or waiting at synchronization points.

Rbin = ∑events duration(event interval ∩ one-second bin)
8 GPUs × 1,000ms = 8,000 GPU-ms of simple device-time per wall second
The 5,312.47 GPU-ms non-NCCL peak therefore means about 5.31 GPU-seconds accumulated across eight devices in that bin, not 5.31 elapsed seconds. Concurrent intervals can in principle exceed 8,000 GPU-ms; this run's peak does not.

Why both traces spike near the beginning

The NCCL peak at profiler t=16.0s is only 4.62s into the client run; the non-NCCL peak at t=18.0s is 6.62s in. During this interval users are still ramping toward 16, and the 14 prompt records are entering the radix cache for the first time. The final previously unseen prompt is first sent at client t=7.520s. That creates a cold first-pass compute wave while PP stages fill, then a downstream SendRecv wait ladder while stages synchronize. The peaks are expected startup shape, not evidence that one kernel consumed several wall seconds.

Peak binAggregatePer-device evidenceInterpretation
Non-NCCL t=18.0s5,312.47 GPU-msGPU 0: 534ms, GPU 1: 743ms, GPU 2: 935ms, GPU 3: 844ms, GPU 4: 842ms, GPU 5: 640ms, GPU 6: 467ms, GPU 7: 307ms3,203.61ms FP8 GEMM plus 1,126.39ms attention during ramp-up.
NCCL t=16.0s3,600.09 GPU-msGPU 0: 39ms, GPU 1: 61ms, GPU 2: 98ms, GPU 3: 339ms, GPU 4: 519ms, GPU 5: 692ms, GPU 6: 852ms, GPU 7: 1,000msA downstream PP wait ladder, not 3.6 elapsed seconds of network transfer.
NCCL evidence. In the t=16.0s peak bin, downstream devices contribute progressively longer SendRecv intervals, culminating in 1,000ms on GPU 7. At the same time non-NCCL work declines toward downstream stages. This is pipeline fill/drain synchronization residency, not 3.6 seconds of payload transmission.

One event interval, step by step

A kernel name identifies GPU code. A kernel event is one execution of that code, with its own device, start, end, and duration. The event-count chart assigns the event to its start second; residency is split across every second the interval overlaps.

ItemObservedMeaning
Kernel namencclDevKernel_SendRecv143,156 separate events share this name.
One eventGPU 7One execution instance on one device.
Start / endt=15.928282s / t=17.044097sGPU event interval, represented as [start, end).
Full duration1,115.815491msEnd minus start for this event.
t=15-16s71.718000msOnly the interval portion inside this one-second bin is counted.
t=16-17s1,000.000000msOnly the interval portion inside this one-second bin is counted.
t=17-18s44.097000msOnly the interval portion inside this one-second bin is counted.
Counts use different units. Nsight recorded 11,330,223 per-device GPU events. CUDA runtime launch APIs recorded 8,518,217 host calls. The former drives the GPU timeline; the latter is independent host-side evidence.
CUDA runtime APICallsAggregate CPU API time
cudaLaunchKernel_v70004,902,41224.624s
cudaLaunchKernelExC_v110603,615,80519.742s

Complete Scheduler Evidence

No unavailable region. The external raw-CRI capture began before smoke, followed the container log through its one rotation, and remained active through the completion gate. The measured run reconciles exactly: 4,282 Receive rows and 4,282 Finish rows for 4,282 client requests.

PP=8 emitted 22,024 stage rows, exactly 2,753 on each PP stage. Logical batch and token totals use only PP0's 2,753 rows; summing PP0-PP7 would count the same logical pipeline work eight times. PP0 recorded 508,736 new and 90,036,928 cached prefill tokens. The complete log contains 0 Decode batch rows.

Capture Lifecycle

Nsight used delayed collection and no fixed duration. Collection was active before issuance. The 300-second issue window drained completely; then request rows and full scheduler counts were validated, all ranks were confirmed idle, and only then was profiling stopped. SQLite export completed synchronously before server shutdown.

GateObserved time (+08:00)
profile start requested2026-07-10T14:24:41.314931078+08:00
collection active2026-07-10T14:24:44.953453600+08:00
benchmark first issuance2026-07-10T14:24:56.280551+08:00
client drain and result write complete2026-07-10T14:29:56.650071745+08:00
completion gate passed2026-07-10T14:30:37.682350168+08:00
profiler stop requested2026-07-10T14:30:47.511243277+08:00
profiler export complete2026-07-10T14:42:42.402192456+08:00
server shutdown complete2026-07-10T14:43:10.968364045+08:00
external log capture stopped2026-07-10T14:43:27.711654985+08:00

Data Quality And Evidence

  • 4,282 persisted request rows with complete session/prompt trace.
  • All requests HTTP 200 with exactly one completion token.
  • Complete CRI stream captured externally across one log rotation.
  • Receive and Finish scheduler counts reconcile exactly to client rows.
  • All eight PP stages have 2,753 prefill rows.
  • Zero Decode batch rows and zero post-run decode sequence length.
  • 11.33M raw kernel events normalized into 364 bins.
  • Peak bins reconciled from per-device raw SQLite intervals.
  • Embedded JSON is raw, valid, and byte-equivalent to the sidecar object.
  • Only gpu-worker-204 was targeted; nodes 201-203 were untouched.

Scope. This is standalone prefill-shaped serving with max_new_tokens=1. It does not exercise a real disaggregated PD bootstrap or KV-transfer path. Prompt reuse and radix-cache hits are intentionally part of this standard workload.
